reasoning: The informal statement asserts that any group G of order 2907 is not simple. The Lean theorem takes an arbitrary type G with `[Fintype G] [Group G]` and hypothesis `card G = 2907`, concluding `¬ IsSimpleGroup G`. The Mathlib `IsSimpleGroup` class (in Mathlib/GroupTheory/Subgroup/Simple.lean) is defined as a `Nontrivial` group whose only normal subgroups are ⊥ or ⊤, which matches the standard textbook definition of a simple group. The cardinality condition matches |G| = 2907, and `Fintype G` together with `card G = 2907` correctly captures "G is finite of order 2907". This is a strictly faithful formalization.
